title: Parliamentary Committees
lead: ==‘Congress in session is Congress on public exhibition, whilst Congress in committee rooms is Congress at work’== — Woodrow Wilson. Standing committees (==DRSCs, financial, others==) and ad-hoc committees (bill- and issue-based) are where scrutiny actually happens.

items.title: Importance
bullets: ==Detailed scrutiny== beyond what the floor allows

items.title: Triggers
bullets: ==Erosion of floor debates== — disruptions, Zero Hour pandemonium
Proliferating schemes (PMAY, Ayushman Bharat, JJM) needing ==budget & performance review==
Weak Opposition — committees as ==alternate scrutiny channels==

items.label: of Bills referred to committees in the 17th LS — down sharply; only 14 Bills so far
DRSC tenure — limiting continuity and specialisation
median time for 50% of bill reports in the 17th LS
meetings of the Joint Committee on the Personal Data Protection Bill (2+ years)

blocks.text: The ==Constitutional Amendment Bill on Simultaneous Elections== was referred to a ==Joint Parliamentary Committee (2025)==.

items: ==Low referral rate== of Bills; ==absenteeism (~50%)==; ==one-year DRSC tenure==.
MPs may lack technical expertise; ==partisan behaviour== inside committees.
==Non-binding recommendations==; inadequate research staff and resources.

items.title: DRSCs
bullets: In-depth scrutiny of ministry policies, budgets, Bills — ==sectoral expertise==
Reduce the burden on the full House; make officials ==justify decisions==
Way forward: close ==pre-budget scrutiny of Demands for Grants==; performance monitoring; ==automatic bill referral==; review of law implementation; ==leadership training ground== for backbenchers

items.title: PAC & Estimates Committee
bullets: ==PAC==: examines ==CAG audit reports==; checks irregularities and compliance
==EC==: scrutinises budget estimates — are expenditures justified and aligned with priorities?
Case studies: PAC exposed ==CWG 2010== irregularities and defence-land misuse (==₹838 crore== lease recovery); EC flagged imported-uranium over-dependence
Committee wins: ==Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Bill 2016== reforms; Doklam preparedness flagged by External Affairs Committee; Railways dividend waiver (2015→2016)

items: ==Institutional==: mandatory bill referral (UK); codified report-reading time before budget votes; a ==Constitution Committee== for CA review; merge overlapping committees (PAC/COPU with DRSCs)
==Functional==: train MPs in budget literacy; professionalise staff; ==performance metrics== (reports per sitting, recommendation quality)
==Procedural==: floor discussion of major reports; a committee calendar (==3-day Parliament, 2-day committee== model)
==Technological==: real-time dashboards integrated with CAG reports; ==live citizen feedback== on pending Bills

blocks.text: OECD: countries with strong committee systems show ==better governance indicators, lower budgetary leakages and more informed legislation==. ‘Maximum Governance’ requires ==Maximum Accountability==.
Reforming the committee system is not an option; it is a democratic imperative. For in the invisible rooms of committee deliberation lies the real defence of our visible democracy.
